title: How the Red Sun Rose: The Origins and Development of the Yan'an Rectification Movement, 1930-1945
description: How the Red Sun Rose: The Origins and Development of the Yan'an Rectification Movement, 1930-1945 is a history by Gao Hua. Banned for exploring in detail Mao Zedong in the Yan'an Rectification and the internal struggles of the CCP.
descriptionParagraphs: How the Red Sun Rose: The Origins and Development of the Yan'an Rectification Movement, 1930-1945 is a history by Gao Hua. Banned for exploring in detail Mao Zedong in the Yan'an Rectification and the internal struggles of the CCP.

What makes it interesting is the way a book becomes legible to officials as a political instrument rather than a neutral cultural object. As a history, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in China. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned for exploring in detail Mao Zedong in the Yan'an Rectification and the internal struggles of the CCP. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: How the Red Sun Rose: The Origins and Development of the Yan'an Rectification Movement, 1930-1945 is a history by Gao Hua. Banned for exploring in detail Mao Zedong in the Yan'an Rectification and the internal struggles of the CCP.
banningParagraphs: How the Red Sun Rose: The Origins and Development of the Yan'an Rectification Movement, 1930-1945 entered censorship debates as a history associated with politics, public argument, and state power. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political control and political dissent.

The earliest event currently captured here is 20th-21st century in China, where Chinese state censors and party authorities banned or suppressed publication. Banned for exploring in detail Mao Zedong in the Yan'an Rectification and the internal struggles of the CCP. Banned for exploring in detail Mao Zedong in the Yan'an Rectification and the internal struggles of the CCP.

counterReadings.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
counterReadings.author: Hannah Arendt
counterReadings.note: A foundational analysis of state terror, propaganda, and ideological conformity.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: A short modern guide to resisting authoritarian politics and controlled public discourse.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
